content.text:
Memory stage: NoteConstructor v1.
        Current time: <instant>
        Timezone: Asia/Jerusalem
        Namespace: project:<0>

        Router decision: MIXED
        Router memory types: CLAIM, NOTE, SOURCE, ENTITY
        Router reason: Document ingest uses deterministic document truth route; stable document facts are extracted as claims, notes/sources keep richer context, and action items stay explicit-only.

        Stage instructions:
        You are NoteConstructor v1 for a long-term AI agent.

Goal:
Construct reusable semantic notes from TARGET_MESSAGE when the material is contextual rather than a clean atomic fact.

A note is for decisions, rationale, design direction, plans, hypotheses, lessons, trade-offs, troubleshooting procedures, and compact document/context digests.
A note must add reusable meaning. It must not be a generic summary of the target turn.

Return JSON:
{
  "notes": [
    {
      "title": "short English title",
      "summary": "self-contained English semantic fragment",
      "note_type": "decision | direction | hypothesis | plan | lesson | doc_digest | context",
      "scope_json": {
        "kind": "global | conversation | entity | environment | document | project",
        "text": "human-readable applicability boundary",
        "basis": "explicit | inferred | summarized | imported"
      },
      "entity_refs": [
        {"entity_id": "resolved-entity-id", "role": "primary | secondary | mentioned | owner | subject"}
      ],
      "keywords": ["short search keyword"],
      "tags": ["short_tag"],
      "candidate_claim_hints": [],
      "confidence": 0.0,
      "importance": 1,
      "valid_from": "ISO-8601 or null",
      "valid_to": "ISO-8601 or null",
      "evidence_quote": "exact substring from TARGET_MESSAGE",
      "evidence_kind": "direct | summarized | imported | inferred | derived_from_note",
      "evidence_reason": "why this source supports the note",
      "rationale": "why this should be a note, not a claim"
    }
  ]
}

Rules:
- Write title, summary, keywords, tags, evidence_reason, and rationale in English.
- Preserve proper names, product names, repo names, file names, exact commands, exact identifiers, dates, and quoted terms.
- Use only entity IDs listed in Resolved entities.
- Never write raw labels such as "user", "assistant", "project", or "document" into entity_refs. If a note is about the user, use the resolved USER entity id from Resolved entities.
- For project-level notes without a more specific subject, use the resolved namespace/project subject entity.
- Use TARGET_MESSAGE as the only evidence. Earlier messages are context only.
- evidence_quote must be an exact short substring copied from TARGET_MESSAGE source data.
- Do not construct notes from greetings, phatic chatter, pure questions, one-off commands with no durable rationale, or memory mechanics discussion that contains no reusable project/user decision.
- Do not construct notes from a single weak uncertain factual observation when source recall is enough and there is no rationale, decision, plan, lesson, or reusable analysis.
- Do not duplicate a claim candidate as a note when a precise claim is enough.
- Do use a note when the target captures "why", "how", "we decided", "current approach", trade-offs, working agreements, troubleshooting order, or a design direction.
- If TARGET_MESSAGE is a document or document section, preserve document title/source/section names and create section-scoped notes for important definitions, component roles, workflows, policies, and lists.
- For dense technical documents and prompt packs, prefer several strong section-scoped notes over one broad document summary. Keep only reusable notes; do not summarize every paragraph.
- For one document section, return at most two notes. If a section defines one compact component or policy, return one note.
- If source metadata force_memory_write=true, create a note when the content is reusable context but not a clean claim/action item/profile update.
- For named components, include the exact component names in title, summary, keywords, or tags so later retrieval can find the component role.
- A troubleshooting/procedure note is allowed, but do not create an action item unless the target creates a commitment.
- Keep each note self-contained: future recall should understand it without the original conversation.
- Keep summary concise: usually one or two sentences.
- Prefer zero notes over broad summary spam.
- Return valid JSON only.

        Relevant persisted memory for dedup/context only:
        - note hot-path:note:<0> DOC_DIGEST; v3 memory data model layered architecture; The `v3` memory data model is designed to support typed memory, lineage, temporal truth, reconcilable updates, richer semantic context, lifecycle/retention, and repairability by separating memory into layers: `Source` as evidence, `Entity`/`Claim` as graph-shaped semantics, `Note` as rich intermediate context, `Profile`/`Task`/`Episode` as operational projections, and `MemoryRun` as audit.; scope=Document section `02. Data Model / 1. Общая идея .. 2. Общие cross-cutting поля` in `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`, describing the overall v3 memory data model architecture.
- claim hot-path:claim:<0> anchors_memory_items; In the v3 memory data model, Entity is an anchor for claims, tasks, notes, and episodes.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md section 3.2 `Entity`
- note hot-path:note:<1> DOC_DIGEST; Note represents durable intermediate semantic context; In `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`, `Note` is an intermediate semantic memory fragment for important retrieval-useful context that should not yet be hardened into final claims, such as design directions, decision rationale, evolving plans, hypotheses, lessons, doc digests, and contextual framing.; scope=Document section `02. Data Model / 3. Главные сущности / 3.4 `Note`` in `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`, defining when to use `Note` and how it differs from Source, Claim, and chain-of-thought.
- note hot-path:note:<2> DOC_DIGEST; Entity is the canonical memory node for aliases, merges, and semantic anchors; In `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`, `Entity` is the canonical memory node that unifies multiple mentions of the same object, anchors claims/tasks/notes/episodes, and supports aliases and merge. Its invariants require merges to preserve history, aliases to point to the current canonical entity, and mistaken merges to be reversible through an audit trail.; scope=Document section `02. Data Model / 3. Главные сущности / 3.2 `Entity`` in `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`, defining the role and invariants of `Entity`.
- claim hot-path:claim:<1> must_not_be_source_final_claim_or_chain_of_thought; In the v3 memory data model, Note must not be treated as a Source, a final Claim, or chain-of-thought.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md section 3.4 Note
- note hot-path:note:<3> DOC_DIGEST; Note and Claim serve different memory roles; In `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`, `Claim` is for precise factual answers, predicate-rule reconciliation, profile updates, and time-aware status answers, while `Note` is for richer retrieval, decision history, rationale, partial or evolving meaning, and later consolidation. The document warns that storing everything as `Claim` creates false precision, while storing everything as `Note` makes memory too soft.; scope=Document section `02. Data Model / 4. Почему `Note` не заменяет `Claim`` in `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`, explaining why Note does not replace Claim.
- note hot-path:note:<4> DOC_DIGEST; Note invariants require one self-contained semantic fragment; `Note` invariants in `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md` require each note to be one self-contained semantic fragment that adds value beyond duplicating the source; `candidate_claims_json` is only hints, and a note may remain long-lived without becoming a claim if it stays useful for retrieval.; scope=Document section `02. Data Model / 3. Главные сущности / 3.4 `Note`` in `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`, describing Note invariants and lifecycle expectations.
- note hot-path:note:<5> DOC_DIGEST; Recommended schema field semantics for Note, Claim, Task, and Profile; In `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`, recommended Pydantic/schema descriptions define how memory fields should constrain behavior: `Note.summary` must be self-contained and preserve uncertainty, `scope_json` prevents leakage or over-generalization, `Claim.normalized_text` is a stand-alone factual statement, `Claim.retrieval_state` is search temperature distinct from truth status, `Task.acceptance_criteria_json` is observable completion criteria, and `Profile.soft_signals` should remain low-confidence or omitted.; scope=Document section `02. Data Model / 7. Recommended field descriptions для schema / Pydantic` in `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`, listing recommended schema descriptions for Note, Claim, Task, and Profile fields.
- claim hot-path:claim:<2> does_not_replace; In the v3 memory data model, Note does not replace Claim because they serve different purposes.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md section 4, why Note does not replace Claim
- note hot-path:note:<6> DOC_DIGEST; Profile is a short rebuildable always-in-context projection; In `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`, `Profile` is a compact always-in-context projection over explicit user instructions, stable claims, and selected long-lived context, not a source of truth. Its invariants emphasize keeping it short, prioritizing explicit facts over inference, leaving disputed facts in claim/note, and making the profile rebuildable.; scope=Document section `02. Data Model / 3. Главные сущности / 3.6 `Profile`` in `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`, defining the role, structure, and invariants of `Profile`.
- note hot-path:note:<7> DOC_DIGEST; PredicateCatalog configures predicate reconciliation behavior; In `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`, `PredicateCatalog` is not a memory item; it is a reconciliation configuration that captures per-predicate differences such as cardinality, temporal semantics, conflict behavior, and whether facts sync into profile or task projections.; scope=Document section `02. Data Model / 3. Главные сущности / 3.3 `PredicateCatalog`` in `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`, defining the role and recommended fields of `PredicateCatalog`.
- claim hot-path:claim:<3> must_not_disappear_silently; A MemoryRun run log must not silently disappear.; scope=Applies to MemoryRun run logs in the v3 memory data model described in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md.
- claim hot-path:claim:<4> must_not_be_mixed_with; truth status and retrieval_state are separate axes in the v3 memory data model and must not be mixed.; scope=Applies to truth status and retrieval_state semantics in the v3 memory data model document.
- claim hot-path:claim:<5> must_not_store_raw_chain_of_thought; In the v3 memory data model, Episode must not store raw chain-of-thought.; scope=Applies to Episode storage rules in the v3 memory data model.
- claim hot-path:claim:<6> plain_question_not_task; In the v3 memory data model, a plain question is not a Task.; scope=Applies to Task classification in the v3 memory data model.
- claim hot-path:claim:<7> represents_lesson_not_reasoning_trace; In the v3 memory data model, Episode represents a lesson, not a full reasoning trace.; scope=Applies to Episode semantics in the v3 memory data model.
- claim hot-path:claim:<8> excludes_disputed_facts; In the v3 memory data model, disputed facts should remain in Claim or Note rather than be pulled into Profile.; scope=Applies to Profile invariants in the v3 memory data model described in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md.
- claim hot-path:claim:<9> has_data_model_layer; The v3 memory data model has a graph-shaped semantic layer that includes Claim.; scope=Applies to the v3 memory data model described in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md.
- claim hot-path:claim:<10> has_data_model_layer; The v3 memory data model has a graph-shaped semantic layer that includes Entity.; scope=Applies to the v3 memory data model described in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md.
- claim hot-path:claim:<11> request_not_commitment; In the v3 memory data model, a request is not a commitment.; scope=Applies to Task classification and creation in the v3 memory data model.

        Resolved entities:
        - mention=Source; action=LINK_EXISTING; id=entity:<0>; type=existing; name=Source; reason=Mention matches existing memory data model concept Source.
- mention=Entity; action=LINK_EXISTING; id=entity:<1>; type=existing; name=Entity; reason=Mention matches existing memory data model concept Entity.
- mention=Note; action=LINK_EXISTING; id=entity:<2>; type=existing; name=Note; reason=Mention matches existing memory data model concept Note.
- mention=Claim; action=LINK_EXISTING; id=entity:<3>; type=existing; name=Claim; reason=Mention matches existing memory data model concept Claim.
- mention=Profile; action=LINK_EXISTING; id=entity:<4>; type=existing; name=Profile; reason=Mention matches existing memory data model concept Profile.
- mention=Task; action=CREATE_NEW; id=entity:<5>; type=CONCEPT; name=Task (memory data model); reason=Target section defines Task as a memory data model concept, and no existing Task candidate is present.
- mention=Episode; action=LINK_EXISTING; id=entity:<6>; type=existing; name=Episode; reason=Mention matches existing memory data model concept Episode.
- mention=MemoryRun; action=LINK_EXISTING; id=entity:<7>; type=existing; name=MemoryRun; reason=Mention matches existing memory data model concept MemoryRun.
- mention=current project; action=CREATE_NEW; id=entity:<8>; type=PROJECT; name=Current project; reason=Default current-project subject for claims without a more specific resolved subject

        Retrieval plan context:
        entity_queries=02. Data Model|agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md|02. Data Model / 8. Модель данных в одной строке|MARKDOWN
        text_queries=02. Data Model agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md 02. Data Model / 8. Модель данных в одной строке MARKDOWN Document title: 02. Data Model Document source: agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md Document section: 02. Data Model / 8. Модель данных в одной строке Document lines: 619-628 ## 8. Модель данных в одной строке - `Source` = что было сказано / увидено - `Entity` = кто/что это вообще - `Note` = что уже важно и полезно, но еще не хочется цементировать как факт - `Claim` = что мы считаем нормализованным фактом - `Profile` = компактная проекция устойчивого контекста - `Task` = что должно быть сделано - `Episode` = чему система научилась - `MemoryRun` = как именно память обновлялась
        predicate_hints=

        [external:document-section:8fa55469a066ca10b666d96d6b7c1f26] EXTERNAL
Source metadata: source_kind=document; origin=pasted_document_section; document_type=MARKDOWN; source_ref=agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md; title=02. Data Model; heading=02. Data Model / 8. Модель данных в одной строке; imported_at=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z
Document title: 02. Data Model
Document source: agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md
Document section: 02. Data Model / 8. Модель данных в одной строке
Document lines: 619-628

## 8. Модель данных в одной строке

- `Source` = что было сказано / увидено
- `Entity` = кто/что это вообще
- `Note` = что уже важно и полезно, но еще не хочется цементировать как факт
- `Claim` = что мы считаем нормализованным фактом
- `Profile` = компактная проекция устойчивого контекста
- `Task` = что должно быть сделано
- `Episode` = чему система научилась
- `MemoryRun` = как именно память обновлялась

responseFormat.description: Construct contextual memory note candidates from one target message.
responseFormat.name: memory_note_constructor

systemPrompts:
You are a concise assistant inside a Gromozeka memory e2e test.
Answer user questions directly.
Do not use tools.
If the question asks for only a short value, return only that value.

You are a concise assistant inside a Gromozeka memory e2e test.
Answer user questions directly.
Do not use tools.
If the question asks for only a short value, return only that value.

## 8. Модель данных в одной строке

- `Source` = что было сказано / увидено
- `Entity` = кто/что это вообще
- `Note` = что уже важно и полезно, но еще не хочется цементировать как факт
- `Claim` = что мы считаем нормализованным фактом
- `Profile` = компактная проекция устойчивого контекста
- `Task` = что должно быть сделано
- `Episode` = чему система научилась
- `MemoryRun` = как именно память обновлялась
